meta.one_liner: Chronic IL-23/IL-17-driven skin disease; a mature, biologics-led market shifting to oral targeted therapy.
meta.sales_note: Product sales are most-recent full-year (FY2025) company-reported franchise totals across ALL indications — not psoriasis-only. PASI figures are cross-trial (NMA-anchored) unless a head-to-head is noted.

epi.headline: ~125M worldwide · ~7.5M US adults · ~20% moderate-to-severe
epi.comorbidities: Elevated cardiovascular, metabolic-syndrome, IBD and depression risk; psoriatic arthritis in ~30%.
journey: Step-up to oral systemics / biologics for moderate-to-severe
Biologic maintenance; switching on loss of response / comorbidity
epi.unmet: Large share of moderate-to-severe patients remain untreated or under-treated (cost, access, injection aversion). Durable drug-free remission and hard-to-treat sites remain the key gaps.
epi.dlqi: Mean DLQI ~8–12 in moderate-to-severe disease — a large quality-of-life impact, comparable to that of cancer or heart disease.
epi.economic: US direct + indirect burden estimated >$100B/yr, roughly half from lost work productivity and comorbidity care.
epi.drug_survival: Real-world persistence is highest for IL-23 inhibitors (~1-yr drug survival ~85–90%) and lowest for TNF inhibitors — a key durability differentiator beyond trial PASI.
epi.mortality: Not directly fatal, but severe psoriasis carries excess cardiovascular mortality — the basis for treat-to-target inflammation control.

sites.note: High-visibility, itch-driven; foams/solutions; a common driver of systemic step-up.
Slow to respond; often needs systemic therapy; predicts psoriatic arthritis.
Painful, disabling, frequently refractory to standard therapy.
Major QoL impact; sensitive-site tolerability; historically understudied.
Growing label set (etanercept, ustekinumab, ixekizumab, secukinumab, apremilast, icotrokinra ≥12).

deals.note: Oral TYK2 TAK-279 acquired from Nimbus.
Otezla divested during the BMS–Celgene merger.

soc.label: Topical therapy
soc.note: First line for mild / localised disease (~80% of patients). Steroid-sparing non-steroidals now extend to face, folds and scalp.

soc.label: Phototherapy
soc.note: Moderate disease or topical-refractory; office- or home-based. Safe in pregnancy; limited by access and time burden.

soc.label: Conventional systemics
soc.note: Moderate-to-severe; low-cost and widely used globally. Requires organ-toxicity monitoring; cyclosporine for short bursts.

soc.label: Advanced oral
soc.note: Oral targeted therapy — the fastest-growing tier; increasingly a first-line systemic choice for oral-preferring patients.

soc.label: Biologics
soc.note: Moderate-to-severe; the deepest, most durable clearance (PASI 90–100). IL-23 and IL-17 are the modern anchors.

soc_note: Escalation is not strictly stepwise — moderate-to-severe patients increasingly start advanced oral or biologic therapy directly. Basis: AAD-NPF guidelines of care.

glossary.def: Psoriasis Area and Severity Index — composite 0–72 score of erythema, induration and scaling weighted by body-region area.
Share of patients reaching ≥90% / 100% improvement in PASI from baseline — the modern efficacy bar (PASI 75 was the older standard).
Body Surface Area involved (%); ≥10% (or special sites) marks moderate-to-severe disease.
Dermatology Life Quality Index (0–30) — patient-reported quality-of-life impact; 0/1 means no effect on life.
Dendritic-cell cytokine (p19 + p40 subunits) that sustains pathogenic Th17 cells — the most disease-specific target.
Th17 effector cytokines acting on keratinocytes; blocking them gives the fastest, deepest skin clearance.
IL-23-dependent T-helper subset that produces IL-17 and IL-22.
Intracellular pseudokinase that transduces IL-23 signalling; oral allosteric inhibition (deucravacitinib) avoids the JAK boxed warning.
Kinase→transcription-factor relay beneath cytokine receptors; STAT3 switches on the Th17 programme.
Master transcription factor of Th17 identity; an emerging small-molecule target.
Engineered protein (usually a monoclonal antibody) that neutralises a specific cytokine or receptor; injectable.
Highly similar copy of an off-patent biologic; launches drive steep price competition (Humira, Stelara).
IL-23-specific (p19) vs IL-12/23-shared (p40) subunits; selective p19 blockade proved more effective than p40.
Network meta-analysis — pools many trials to rank therapies never tested head-to-head (e.g. Armstrong 2020).
Real-world persistence: how long patients stay on a therapy before switching/stopping — a durability proxy.
Psoriatic arthritis — inflammatory joint disease developing in ~30% of psoriasis patients.
Patent expiry that opens a product to generic or biosimilar competition.

trials.result: First IL-17A pivotal; PASI 90 ~59%, superior to etanercept.
IL-17RA blockade; very high clearance; a boxed warning later shaped uptake.
PASI 90 ~68–71% — among the fastest, deepest responders of its era.
IL-23 p19; PASI 90 ~73% at wk16, superior to adalimumab.
IL-23 p19; established q12w dosing; PASI 90 ~35–39%.
PASI 90 ~75%, superior to ustekinumab — set the IL-23 benchmark.
Head-to-head win: guselkumab beat secukinumab on wk-48 PASI 90 (durability).

trials.trial: BE VIVID / BE READY / BE SURE
trials.result: Dual IL-17A/F; PASI 100 ~60% — the deepest-clearance benchmark to date.
First oral TYK2; PASI 75 ~58% vs apremilast ~35% — opened the oral-targeted era.
Next-gen oral TYK2: >50% PASI 90 — raising the oral efficacy bar.
First oral IL-23R peptide; positioned as a first-line systemic.

market_note: Directional shares of the branded systemic/biologic market — illustrative, not audited. IL-23 & IL-17 lead; oral targeted therapy is the fastest-growing slice.

attrs.safety: Cleanest profile — no increased candidiasis or IBD signal (may be IBD-protective), low serious-infection rates, no boxed warnings.
Mucocutaneous Candida (highest with bimekizumab); caution/avoid in IBD; brodalumab carries a boxed warning for suicidal ideation. No consistent MACE/malignancy signal.
Boxed warnings: serious infections (incl. TB reactivation — screen first) and malignancy (lymphoma). HBV reactivation, demyelination, CHF worsening; highest herpes-zoster rate.

Selective TYK2 (deucravacitinib) does NOT carry the JAK boxed warning; 5-yr data show no MACE/VTE/malignancy signal. Classic JAK inhibitors carry the class boxed warning (infection, MACE, thrombosis, malignancy).
No boxed warning, no lab monitoring. GI (diarrhea/nausea), weight loss, and a depression/mood warning (apremilast).
Local, well-tolerated; steroid-sparing. Suitable for chronic use and sensitive sites.
Skin atrophy, striae, tachyphylaxis and HPA-axis suppression with chronic/high-potency use.
Organ toxicity and monitoring: MTX (hepatotoxicity, cytopenia, teratogenic), cyclosporine (nephrotoxicity, hypertension), acitretin (teratogenic, hyperlipidemia).

attrs.note: FY2025 sales all-indication (PsO+PsA+IBD); AbbVie's top product
approved in UC; Ph3 in psoriasis
first oral IL-23R peptide; FDA-approved Mar 2026 (JNJ-2113)

attrs.bios: biosimilars from Jan 2025 (Wezlana, Steqeyma…); sales −41% YoY

attrs.note: dual IL-17A and IL-17F; highest PASI 100; sales +>200% YoY
IL-17A/F Nanobody (M-1095); Ph2 MIRA in PsO, sponsor prioritizing HS

attrs.bios: biosimilars from 2023 (US); sales collapsed from ~$21B peak
biosimilars in EU; none in US (patents ~2029)

attrs.note: PEGylated Fab' (no Fc); used more in arthritis than PsO
studied in Ph3; not approved for psoriasis (US)
FY2025 approx; selected for Medicare IRA price-setting
Ph2b positive in PsO; sponsor advancing Ph3 in atopic dermatitis
FY2025 franchise (PsO+seb derm+AD); scalp/body foam approved May 2025
Ph2 for psoriasis discontinued; approved in MS

attrs.significance: First big psoriasis-biologic patent cliff; ~9 biosimilars in 2023.
First IL-17A and IL-17F inhibitor; deepest skin-clearance benchmark.
~7 biosimilars launch in 2025 at up to ~85% discount; Stelara ~$10.4B (2024).

nodes.label: Zoryve (roflumilast) foam approved for scalp & body

attrs.significance: Extends the topical PDE4 franchise into scalp.
>50% PASI 90, ~30% PASI 100 at wk16; positions oral TYK2 as a 2026 filer.
~74% PASI 75; oral TYK2 competitor; NDA planned H2 2026.
First targeted ORAL IL-23R peptide; category-defining first-line oral.
Longest runway of the IL-23 class; AbbVie's post-Humira anchor.

attrs.note: Oral TYK2 TAK-279 acquired from Nimbus.
Otezla divested during the BMS–Celgene merger.

attrs.description: Dermal antigen-presenting cell. On activation it secretes IL-23, IL-12 and TNF, initiating and sustaining the pathogenic T-cell response that drives psoriatic plaques.
IL-23-dependent effector T cell. It signals intracellularly through TYK2 / JAK-STAT and secretes the effector cytokines IL-17A, IL-17F and IL-22.
Epidermal cell. Under IL-17 and TNF it hyperproliferates and releases further chemokines and cytokines, closing a self-amplifying inflammatory loop that forms the scaly plaque.
The clinical lesion — a well-demarcated, thickened, scaly erythematous plaque produced by keratinocyte hyperproliferation and immune infiltration.
Dendritic-cell cytokine (p19 + p40) that maintains pathogenic Th17 cells — the most disease-specific and best-validated target in psoriasis.
Shared subunit of IL-12 and IL-23. Blocking p40 (ustekinumab) inhibits both; selective p19 blockade has since proven more effective.
The effector cytokines acting on keratinocytes. Neutralising them delivers the fastest, deepest skin clearance of any class.
Th17-derived cytokine promoting keratinocyte proliferation and epidermal acanthosis.
Pleiotropic pro-inflammatory cytokine that amplifies the cascade at multiple points; the first successful biologic target in psoriasis.
Intracellular pseudokinase that transduces IL-23 (and type-I interferon) signalling — the basis of oral allosteric TYK2 inhibition.
Phosphodiesterase-4 regulates intracellular cAMP in immune cells; inhibiting it dampens pro-inflammatory cytokine output (apremilast, roflumilast).
Cell-surface receptor complex (IL-23R + IL-12Rβ1) on Th17 cells. Engaged by IL-23; the target of the first oral IL-23R peptide antagonist (icotrokinra).
Keratinocyte receptor (IL-17RA/RC) for IL-17A/F. Neutralised directly by brodalumab; signals via Act1 to NF-κB.
Janus kinase paired with TYK2 beneath the IL-23 receptor; together they phosphorylate STAT3 on cytokine binding.
Transcription factor phosphorylated by TYK2/JAK2. It translocates to the nucleus and switches on the Th17 genetic programme.
Master transcription factor of Th17 identity, induced downstream of STAT3; it drives IL-17/IL-22 production and is an emerging small-molecule target.
Central inflammatory transcription factor activated in keratinocytes by IL-17 and TNF, amplifying chemokine and cytokine output.
